title: camelina sativa subsp. macrocarpa
intro: Camelina sativa subsp. macrocarpa, also known as large-fruited camelina, is a subspecies of the annual plant Camelina sativa, native to Europe and Western Asia. It has been found to possess various medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which are attributed to its high content of polyunsaturated fatty acids, particularly alpha-linolenic acid. The health benefits of camelina sativa subsp. macrocarpa include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting brain function, as well as exhibiting potential anti-cancer properties. Herbal preparations of camelina sativa subsp. macrocarpa may include oil extraction, which is often used in cosmetics and pharmaceuticals, as well as infusions and tinctures for medicinal purposes.
